steps.title: Compare inner fiber fineness
steps.content: Smaller fiber diameter means softer wool. The table shows the Navajo-Churro's inner fiber reaches 10 microns, while the mouflon's is 15 microns and the Merino's minimum is 17 microns. At its softest, the Navajo-Churro's inner fiber is finer than both.

steps.title: Choose the supported claim
steps.content:
• Choice A: Falsely calls Navajo-Churro inner fiber uniform.
  • Choice B: Contradicts the passage's claim that Merino wool suits delicate garments.
  • Choice C: Overstates softness across both outer and inner fiber.
  • Choice D: States that the Navajo-Churro's softest inner fiber is softer than the mouflon's or Merino's.
The answer is D.
